sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try moving the green center using arrow keys
# First, let me check the current position of the green center
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green center: boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Try moving right
result = action(['RIGHT'])
print("\nAfter RIGHT:")
print("  board_changed:", last_action_result.get('board_changed'))

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green center: boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Try moving left
result = action(['LEFT'])
print("\nAfter LEFT:")
print("  board_changed:", last_action_result.get('board_changed'))

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green center: boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Try moving up
result = action(['UP'])
print("\nAfter UP:")
print("  board_changed:", last_action_result.get('board_changed'))

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green center: boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Try moving down
result = action(['DOWN'])
print("\nAfter DOWN:")
print("  board_changed:", last_action_result.get('board_changed'))

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green center: boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
